channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Judge sides with 16 states, putting on pause Biden's delay of consideration of gas export projects
item.description: LAKE CHARLES, La. (AP) — The Biden administration can’t delay consideration of projects aimed at exporting liquefied natural gas while a legal challenge by 16 Republican-led states plays out in federal court, a Louisiana judge said Monday. U.S. District Judge James Cain, Jr.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Rudy Giuliani disbarred in New York for efforts to subvert 2020 election
item.description: Rudy Giuliani, who once served as New York City’s mayor and the U.S. Attorney for the Southern District of New York, has been disbarred “effective immediately” for his efforts to subvert the 2020 presidential election, a state appeals court ruled Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KRON4

item.title: How to spot the newest ATM skimmers uncovered by St. Louis police
item.description: The latest ATM skimmer device that can clone your bank debit card data is made of thin plastic that’s hidden inside the card reader.
item.dc:creator: Chris Hayes, ABC 4

item.title: Florida prosecutors knew Epstein raped teenage girls 2 years before cutting deal, transcript shows
item.description: Florida prosecutors knew the late millionaire and financier Jeffrey Epstein sexually assaulted teenage girls two years before they cut a plea deal that has long been criticized as too lenient and a missed opportunity to imprison him a decade earlier, according to transcripts released Monday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Prosecutor won't oppose Trump sentencing delay in hush money case after high court immunity ruling
item.description: Manhattan prosecutors said Tuesday they would not oppose Donald Trump’s request to delay the sentencing in his hush money trial as he seeks to have the conviction overturned following a Supreme Court ruling that granted broad immunity protections to presidents.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Texas teen's spinal cord severed after jumping into pool to escape wasp
item.description: SJ Williams, 13, is fighting for his recovery after severing his spinal cord. Williams was running from a wasp when he went head-first into the shallow end of a pool.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump says he can end the Russia-Ukraine war in one day. Russia's UN ambassador says he can't
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — Donald Trump has repeatedly said he could settle the war between Russia and Ukraine in one day if he’s elected president again. Russia’s United Nations ambassador says he can’t.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Supreme Court sidesteps new several new gun cases, including challenge to state assault weapons ban
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court sidestepped a new set of Second Amendment fights Tuesday, declining to hear a challenge to an assault weapons ban in Illinois and sending other gun cases back to lower courts, including a challenge to the law used to convict Hunter Biden.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Supreme Court turns away OSHA challenge over opposition from Thomas, Gorsuch
item.description: Over opposition from two conservative justices, the Supreme Court on Tuesday turned away a challenge that could’ve gutted the Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA).
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Tesla sales fall for second straight quarter despite price cuts, but beat analyst expectations
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — Tesla's global sales fell for the second straight quarter despite price cuts and low-interest financing offers, another sign of weaking demand for the company's products and electric vehicles overall.
item.dc:creator: TOM KRISHER AP Auto Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Final person to plead guilty in Denver fire that killed 5 people from Senegal could get 60 years
item.description: DENVER (AP) — In between shifts at Amazon to earn money she could send home to her relatives in Senegal — working opposite times as her sister-in-law so they could care for each other's children — Hassan Diol would call to talk to her husband several times a day.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US to pay Moderna $176 million to develop pandemic flu vaccine
item.description: The U.S. government will pay the vaccine maker Moderna $176 million to develop a pandemic vaccine that could be used to treat bird flu in people as cases in dairy cows continue to mount across the country, federal officials announced Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Senator wants Washington Commanders to pay tribute to an old logo that offends many Indigenous
item.description: After a half century of activism, many Native Americans thought a bitter debate over the capital's football mascot was over two years ago when the team became the Washington Commanders.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM LEE BREWER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The US will pay Moderna $176 million to develop an mRNA pandemic flu vaccine
item.description: The U.S. government will pay the vaccine maker Moderna $176 million to develop a pandemic vaccine that could be used to treat bird flu in people as cases in dairy cows continue to mount across the country.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Supreme Court won’t review Illinois ban on certain semiautomatic guns
item.description: The Supreme Court declined to hear a challenge to Illinois’s ban on certain semiautomatic weapons and high-capacity magazines Tuesday, avoiding what would’ve been a blockbuster Second Amendment case.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court won't hear tech liability challenge from teen groomed on Snapchat
item.description: The Supreme Court will not consider a challenge to the scope of a federal law immunizing tech companies from liability for their users’ content brought by a teenager who was allegedly groomed by a teacher on Snapchat.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: 20 Disneyland park goers stuck at top of roller coaster when ride malfunctions
item.description: Twenty riders on Disney's Incredicoaster at Anaheim's California Adventure had to be rescued after an unknown malfunction, with temperatures in the 80s.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Red Lobster rolls out Flavor Flav's Faves menu
item.description: A Red Lobster menu full of Flavor Flav's favorite items? Yeah boyeeeeee! The restaurant chain has rolled out a new menu inspired by its biggest celebrity fan.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, KTLA 5

item.title: Groom shot in the head during wedding party robbery attempt
item.description: A wedding celebration quickly turned to tragedy after a group of masked gunmen crashed the reception and shot the groom..
item.dc:creator: Caroline Pettey, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Biden campaign tries to stamp out Democratic calls to drop out
item.description: President Biden’s reelection campaign and Biden's allies are going to great lengths to stamp out questions from Democrats over whether he should drop out of the race after an abysmal debate performance.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Darrell Christian, former AP managing editor and sports editor, dies at 75
item.description: Darrell L. Christian, a former managing editor and sports editor of The Associated Press known for a demanding demeanor and insistence on excellence during more than four decades with the news agency, died Monday. He was 75.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden proposes first-ever nationwide worker protections from heat
item.description: As the nation sizzles, the Biden administration is proposing the nation’s first-ever standards aimed at protecting workers from extreme heat.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: Chicago Pride parade chaos: police attacked and 53 arrested
item.description: More than 50 people were arrested and multiple police officers were injured following Chicago's Pride parade Sunday with reports of people jumping on vehicles and throwing bottles.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Dozens of illegal immigrants accused of border stampede in El Paso, Texas, released into US: ICE
item.description: Dozens of the more than 200 illegal immigrants who stormed the southern border in El Paso, Texas, in March have been released into the U.S., ICE says.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cup Noodles releases new s'mores flavor for summer
item.description: Nissin Foods USA announced they're releasing a new limited-edition Cup Noodles Campfire S'mores flavor of ramen noodles.
item.dc:creator: Drew Miller, KRON4

item.title: Devout athletes find strength in their faith. But practicing it and elite sports can pose hurdles
item.description: It’s been 100 years since a Scottish runner famously refused to race on a Sunday at the Paris Olympics because of his Christian beliefs.
item.dc:creator: Giovanna Dell'Orto And Deepa Bharath, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: White House touts efforts to lower gas prices ahead of July 4 travel blitz
item.description: The Biden administration announced on Tuesday the sale of 1 million barrels of gasoline, making its latest effort to reduce gas prices ahead of the Fourth of July travel blitz.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden administration proposes rule for workplaces to address excessive heat
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Biden administration proposed a new rule Tuesday to address excessive heat in the workplace, as tens of millions of people in the U.S. are under heat advisories due to blistering temperatures. If finalized, the measure would protect an estimated 36 million U.S.
item.dc:creator: SUMAN NAISHADHAM, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How the hot water fueling Hurricane Beryl foretells a scary storm season
item.description: Hurricane Beryl's explosive growth into an unprecedented early whopper of a storm shows the literal hot water the Atlantic and Caribbean are in right now and the kind of season ahead, experts said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump seeks to set aside hush money verdict hours after immunity ruling
item.description: In prior court filings, Trump contended he is immune from prosecution for conduct alleged to involve official acts during his tenure in office.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: All Republican women in South Carolina Senate voted out of office
item.description: The only three Republican women in the South Carolina Senate took on their party and stopped a total abortion ban from passing in their state last year. In return, they lost their jobs.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Hunter Biden sues Fox News over miniseries
item.description: Hunter Biden has sued Fox News over a miniseries it published and then took down from its subscriber streaming service Fox Nation.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, WGN 9

item.title: Supreme Court rules ex-presidents have some immunity, sends Trump case back to lower court
item.description: In a historic 6-3 ruling, the justices said for the first time that former presidents have absolute immunity from prosecution for their official acts and no immunity for unofficial acts.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Costco CEO says this is the most important item the store sells
item.description: Costco warehouses seemingly have everything from the popular $1.50 hot dog combo and affordable gasoline to gold bars and electronics.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Tracking the Tropics: Beryl strengthens into Category 5 hurricane
item.description: The National Hurricane Center is tracking Hurricane Beryl, Tropical Storm Chris and a new tropical wave.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Accettulla, WGN 9

item.title: How the hot water that fueled Hurricane Beryl foretells a scary storm season
item.description: Hurricane Beryl’s explosive growth into an unprecedented early storm shows the literal hot water the Atlantic and Caribbean are in right now and the kind of season ahead.
item.dc:creator: Seth Borenstein, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The Supreme Court rules for a North Dakota truck stop in a new blow to federal regulators
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court opened the door Monday to new, broad challenges to regulations long after they take effect, the third blow in a week to federal agencies.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, Fox 5
